chapter_title.en: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah thirty times a day

text: أَبِی رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی الْأَرْمَنِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عِمْرَانَ الْخَرَّاطِ عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ ثَلَاثِینَ مَرَّهً لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْحَقُّ الْمُبِینُ اسْتَقْبَلَ الْغِنَی وَ اسْتَدْبَرَ الْفَقْرَ وَ قَرَعَ بَابَ الْجَنَّهِ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad b. Hilal from Muhammad b. Isa al-Armani from Abi ’Imran al-Kharrat from al-Awzaa’i from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) who said: Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) narrates from his father, who in turn narrates from his father that a person who recites Laa ilaaha illallaahul h’aqqul mubeen thirty times a day will be given wealth, his poverty will vanish and he will knock at the door of Paradise.”
